statement.markdown: Write a function to fix a MongoDB query projection. The function should take a MongoDB query object and a field name as input, and return a new query object with the specified field added to the projection. If the field already exists in the projection, it should be updated with the correct value. The function should also handle the case where the field is not present in the query object.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a MongoDB query object and a field name as a string.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a new MongoDB query object with the specified field added to the projection.
statement.notes: The function should not modify the original query object.

constraints.description: Number of fields in the query object

examples.explanation: The function should add the specified field to the projection and update its value.
The function should not modify the value of the field if it already exists in the projection.
The function should handle the case where the field is not present in the query object.

hints.text: Check if the field already exists in the projection.
If the field exists, update its value.
If the field does not exist, add it to the projection.

editorial.title: Fix MongoDB Projection
editorial.intuition: The function should iterate over the fields in the query object and update the projection accordingly.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a brute force approach by iterating over all possible fields in the query object.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a recursive function to handle nested fields.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a recursive function to handle nested fields and update the projection accordingly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should be tested with various inputs to ensure it produces the correct output.
editorial.dryRun:
You can test the function with the following inputs: 
"{"projection":{"name":"John","age":25}}"
"name"
"""
